title: Cuzzie's Team Meeting
objective: Weekly operational sync for the Cuzzie's team — align on what happened last week, what's planned for next week, what we did right, and what we did wrong.

carryover.item: Check whether Wednesday's vendor orders (Hamilton Farms, Agricine, MB1/Kai, Fresh, Niche, Love Grow, Elicit, Dime, Harvest Moon, Verono, Abco, Garden State, Heavy Hitters, Garden Society) arrived on time and Memorial Day stocking is on track.
Synthesize feedback from this week's 1:1 check-ins — surface common themes and proposed operational changes.
Begin scoping Phase 3 (mid-June expansion to 8 AM–10 PM, 7 days) staffing and shift structure as Memorial Day push winds down.
Confirm KPI baselines (Google reviews, delivery turnaround, upsells) are measured starting first week of June.

Revisit meeting format — objective only partially met this instance (heavy on next-week plans, light on retrospective). Consider structuring next sync with explicit "last week recap / what worked / what didn't" prompts.

history.objective: Weekly operational sync for the Cuzzie's team — align on what happened last week, what's planned for next week, what we did right, and what we did wrong.
history.objective_met_note: Heavy on next-week strategy and the three-phase plan; light on last-week retrospective and right/wrong evaluation.
history.outcome: Strategic kickoff for the post-funding phase. Lemar framed the next month as three phases — Payback (vendor reset + inventory replenish), Get Back (marketing/customer recovery), Expansion (8 AM–10 PM, 7 days, mid-June). Wednesday funding triggers the first wave of vendor orders targeting Memorial Day stocking; full stock targeted by the Friday after Memorial Day. Joshua announced June KPI tracking, asked the inventory team to standardize product descriptions, proposed a team dinner, and reminded the delivery team on the new-customer cash policy. Annmarie pushed (and got agreement on) tightening the points program before the inventory influx.
decisions: Loyalty program revised: $25 minimum spend for $5 reward, $100 spend for $20 reward — effective before Memorial Day.
Three-phase strategic plan adopted: Payback → Get Back → Expansion (8 AM–10 PM, 7 days, mid-June).
Memorial Day stocking target: full stock by the Friday after Memorial Day.
KPI tracking begins first week of June: Google reviews, delivery turnaround, upsell performance.

tasks.name: Make station picks (3-vote threshold) for Memorial Day stocking
Configure cataloging tools to include product descriptions
Submit updated availability (to Joshua/Lemar, then enter in Gusto)

shortlist.name: Conduct 1:1 check-ins with each team member this week

skipped: Lemar: secure early vendor orders for Wednesday (already in flight)
Delivery team: new-customer cash-not-accepted script (deleted per Lemar)
